text: The water cycle describes how water evaporates from the surface, rises into the atmosphere, condenses into clouds, and falls back as precipitation.
Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants convert sunlight into chemical energy stored as glucose.
The mitochondria are organelles found in eukaryotic cells, often called the powerhouse of the cell because they generate most of the cell's ATP.
Continental drift theory proposes that Earth's continents were once joined as a single supercontinent called Pangaea before slowly moving apart.
Newton's first law of motion states that an object at rest remains at rest, and an object in motion remains in motion, unless acted upon by an external force.
The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 299,792 kilometres per second and is a fundamental constant in physics.
DNA is a double-helix molecule made of four nucleotide bases — adenine, thymine, guanine, and cytosine — that encode genetic information.
The periodic table organises chemical elements by atomic number and groups elements with similar chemical properties into columns.
Plate tectonics explains how the Earth's lithosphere is divided into plates that move and interact, causing earthquakes and volcanic activity.
The human immune system includes both innate immunity, which provides immediate non-specific defences, and adaptive immunity, which mounts targeted responses to specific pathogens.
Black holes are regions of spacetime where gravity is so strong that nothing, not even light, can escape once it crosses the event horizon.
The Amazon rainforest produces roughly 20 percent of the world's oxygen and is home to more than 10 percent of all species on Earth.
Quantum entanglement is a phenomenon where two particles become correlated such that the state of one instantly influences the state of the other, regardless of distance.
The greenhouse effect occurs when atmospheric gases such as carbon dioxide and methane trap heat radiated from Earth's surface, warming the planet.
CRISPR-Cas9 is a gene-editing tool derived from a bacterial immune mechanism that allows precise modifications to DNA sequences.
The theory of general relativity describes gravity as the curvature of spacetime caused by mass and energy.
Ocean currents distribute heat around the planet, regulating climate and influencing weather patterns in coastal regions.
Neurons transmit electrical signals across synapses using neurotransmitters, forming the basis of communication in the nervous system.
The standard model of particle physics classifies all known fundamental particles and describes three of the four fundamental forces.
Fermentation is a metabolic process in which organisms convert sugars into acids, gases, or alcohol in the absence of oxygen.
